vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-863: Workspace + branch hygiene sweep (124 feature/* branches, 28 worktrees)

plan.title: Workspace + branch hygiene sweep (124 feature/* branches, 28 worktrees)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: 194 local feature branches (124 cited in issue + drift since filing), 167 remote feature branches, and 28 workspace worktrees have accumulated. Root cause: `close_out` config defaults `remove_workspace: false` and `delete_feature_branch: false`, so even the human-gated close-out ceremony preserves debris. The 2026-05-25 operator audit caught 9 freshly closed-out issues with stale branches (PAN-1358/1379/1381/1385/1389/1391/1393/1407/1408). `postMergeLifecycle` is correctly NOT deleting branches/worktrees at merge time (the workspace must survive the `verifying_on_main` phase), but no downstream step picks up the deletion either. There is no janitor patrol that catches missed cleanup. Triage-then-act tooling does not exist.
narratives.Proposal:
Three coordinated changes, shipped together:

1. **One-shot sweep tooling** — `pan admin hygiene scan` writes a triage report (`docs/CLEANUP-<date>.md`) classifying every feature/* branch and every workspaces/feature-* worktree against a safety filter. `pan admin hygiene apply --category <cat> [--dry-run]` executes cleanup per operator-approved category, re-running the safety filter immediately before each destructive op.

2. **Lifecycle hardening** — flip `close_out` config defaults to delete (workspace still survives `verifying_on_main`; deletion only at close-out), add a post-condition assertion to `closeOut` that fires `close_out_invariant_violated` if cleanup didn't complete.

3. **Periodic janitor** — low-frequency Deacon patrol hook (every 60min) that runs the same classifier and auto-deletes safe candidates. First run is forced into dry-run; operator must flip `janitor.armed` in app_settings to enable live deletion. Per-cycle cap at 10 deletions.

4. **One-shot execution** — agent runs the new CLI against the live repo and lands the cleaned state in this PR. Expected post-state: ~20 feature branches max, only with active worktrees / open PRs / running agents.

autoDecisions.summary: Sweep tooling lives under `pan admin hygiene` (matches `pan admin cloister/tracker/...` pattern)
autoDecisions.rationale: Existing admin namespace convention; less surface area than a top-level verb.

The operator's 2026-05-25 audit proves the current defaults are causing the very accumulation this issue is trying to solve. Workspace still survives merge → verifying_on_main; deletion only at close-out, which is human-gated.

autoDecisions.summary: Janitor first run is forced into dry-run; operator must flip `janitor.armed` in app_settings to go live
autoDecisions.rationale: Defense against surprise damage on first deploy when hundreds of stale branches exist.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-cycle cap of 10 deletions for the janitor
autoDecisions.rationale: Rate-limit catastrophic damage from a misconfigured safety filter; remaining candidates surface as activity entries for the next cycle.
Catches abandoned work but leaves a window for the agent pipeline to land legitimate work that's mid-flight without an open PR yet (e.g. between merge and close-out).

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse `isBranchMerged` squash-merge detection from src/lib/close-out.ts instead of re-implementing
autoDecisions.rationale: Squash-merge detection is subtle (branch tip not ancestor of main; needs code-diff check ignoring planning artifacts). The existing implementation has been battle-tested by 70+ close-out runs; duplicating it is bug-bait.

autoDecisions.summary: Honor `cleanup:keep` git notes as a permanent do-not-delete pin
autoDecisions.rationale: Operators occasionally want to preserve a branch indefinitely (long-running experiment, historical reference). A git note is durable, greppable, and survives across machines.

autoDecisions.summary: Workspaces matching `workspaces/feature-*` only; other worktree paths flagged for manual review
autoDecisions.rationale: Scope-limit destructive ops to the convention the issue title cites. Non-conforming paths (`feature-agent-min-*`, ad-hoc) are rare and worth a human glance.

autoDecisions.summary: Cap any single scan's gh-CLI cost at one `gh pr list` call, cached for the scan duration
autoDecisions.rationale: Without this, classifying 200+ branches would trigger 200+ gh-CLI calls and hit GitHub's secondary rate limit (~30 req/min for authenticated).

items.title: Pure branch classifier with safety filters

narrative.Action:
Create `src/lib/hygiene/branch-classifier.ts` exporting `classifyBranch(branchName, ctx): Effect.Effect<BranchClassification>` where `BranchClassification = { branch: string; verdict: 'safe-to-delete' | 'keep-open-pr' | 'keep-active-worktree' | 'keep-active-agent' | 'keep-protected' | 'keep-young' | 'keep-pinned' | 'manual-review'; reasons: string[]; mergedSha?: string }`. The function must:

1. Reject protected names (main, master, the current branch in any worktree).
2. Consult a pre-cached `Map<string, OpenPr>` (PRs fetched once per scan via `gh pr list --state open --json headRefName,number,isDraft`) — if present, verdict is `keep-open-pr`.
3. Cross-check against `listWorktrees(repoPath)` — if a worktree references this branch, verdict is `keep-active-worktree`.
4. Cross-check against `tmux -L panopticon list-sessions` output via `listSessionNames()` — if `agent-<issue-from-branch>` or `planning-<issue-from-branch>` is running, verdict is `keep-active-agent`.
5. Cross-check the branch's last-commit date against `cleanup.min_age_days` (default 7) — younger = `keep-young`.
6. Cross-check `git notes show <branch> 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cleanup:keep$'` — if pinned, verdict is `keep-pinned`.
7. Call `isBranchMerged` semantics (reuse `src/lib/close-out.ts:isBranchMerged` — DO NOT re-implement squash-merge detection) — if merged AND none of the above blocked, verdict is `safe-to-delete` with `mergedSha` populated for reflog recovery.
8. Recognise slot branches (`feature/<parent>-slot-N`, see `parseSlotBranch` in merge-agent.ts) and only treat them safe-to-delete iff parent feature branch is itself merged AND already deleted.
9. Anything else (e.g. orphaned, no PR, not merged, > 30 days old): `manual-review` with reasons listing every signal checked.

Classifier must be a pure function over a `ClassifierContext` (open-pr cache, worktree list, tmux session names, current time) — no side effects, no fs writes outside the read-only git/gh commands needed to populate the context.
narrative.Tests: Unit tests with mocked context covering every verdict branch; integration test against a small fixture repo with three branches (one merged, one open-pr, one young) and a stub `gh` mock.

subItems.title: `classifyBranch` returns `keep-protected` for main/master/current-branch in every worktree
Squash-merge detection reuses `isBranchMerged` from src/lib/close-out.ts; classifier file does NOT contain its own `git diff main...` command
Slot branches (`feature/pan-1201-slot-2`) get `manual-review` until their parent feature branch is merged + deleted, then `safe-to-delete`
`cleanup:keep` git note pins a branch to `keep-pinned` regardless of any other signal
Function is pure: callable repeatedly with the same context returns identical verdicts; no fs writes or network calls inside `classifyBranch` itself
Non-PAN-prefixed branches (`feature/473`, `feature/agent-min-841`) classify by branch name only — no tracker-issue lookup is attempted

items.title: Pure worktree classifier with safety filters

narrative.Action:
Create `src/lib/hygiene/worktree-classifier.ts` exporting `classifyWorktree(worktree: WorktreeInfo, ctx): Effect.Effect<WorktreeClassification>` where `WorktreeClassification = { path: string; branch: string; verdict: 'safe-to-remove' | 'keep-active-agent' | 'keep-uncommitted' | 'keep-non-feature' | 'keep-branch-not-merged' | 'manual-review'; reasons: string[]; dirty: boolean; uncommittedFiles?: string[] }`. The function must:

1. Reject worktrees whose path does not match `workspaces/feature-*` — verdict is `keep-non-feature` (flagged for manual review).
2. Run `git -C <worktreePath> status --porcelain` — non-empty output means `keep-uncommitted` with `uncommittedFiles` populated. Apply CLI will stash these with the `salvageable:` prefix.
3. Check `tmux -L panopticon list-sessions` for `agent-<issue>` or `planning-<issue>` matching the worktree's issue — running = `keep-active-agent`.
4. Defer to `classifyBranch` (passed in via ctx, not re-imported — avoids circular dep) for the underlying branch verdict. If branch verdict is `safe-to-delete`, worktree verdict is `safe-to-remove`. If branch is `keep-young`, worktree is `manual-review` (don't preemptively kill a young workspace).
5. Recognise the `WorktreeInfo.prunable` flag from `listWorktrees` — prunable worktrees are admin-dir-only leftovers and always `safe-to-remove`.

Like the branch classifier, no side effects beyond read-only git commands. Stash creation lives in the apply CLI, not in the classifier.
narrative.Tests: Unit tests with mocked context per verdict; fixture-repo integration test confirming prunable detection.

subItems.title: Worktrees outside `workspaces/feature-*` always get `keep-non-feature`
Dirty worktrees (`git status --porcelain` non-empty) get `keep-uncommitted` with the file list populated
Worktrees whose branch classifier verdict is `keep-young` get `manual-review` (NOT `safe-to-remove`)
Prunable worktrees (admin-dir leftover) always get `safe-to-remove`
Worktree classifier does NOT import the branch classifier directly — branch verdict is passed via context

narrative.Action:
Create `src/lib/hygiene/report.ts` exporting `generateCleanupReport(projectRoot: string, today: Date): Effect.Effect<{ path: string; markdown: string; summary: ReportSummary }>`. Output schema:

```md
# Workspace + Branch Hygiene Sweep — YYYY-MM-DD

## Summary
| Verdict | Branches | Worktrees |
|---|---|---|
| safe-to-delete / safe-to-remove | N | M |
| keep-open-pr | … | — |
| keep-active-agent | … | … |
| keep-active-worktree | … | — |
| keep-uncommitted | — | … |
| keep-young | … | … |
| keep-pinned | … | — |
| manual-review | … | … |

## Branches
### safe-to-delete (N)
- feature/pan-1358 [merged @ <sha7>; PR #1403]
- …
### keep-open-pr (N)
- …
## Worktrees
### safe-to-remove (N)
- workspaces/feature-pan-1391 [branch feature/pan-1391 safe-to-delete]
- …
## Generated by `pan admin hygiene scan` at <iso-timestamp>
```

Writes to `<projectRoot>/docs/CLEANUP-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md` and returns the path. Idempotent — same input on same day overwrites the same file.
narrative.Tests: Snapshot test on a fixture context with one branch in each verdict + one worktree in each verdict.

subItems.title: Output file written to `docs/CLEANUP-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md` with today's date
Summary table at top shows counts per verdict for both branches and worktrees
Each `safe-to-delete` branch entry includes the merged SHA (short) for reflog recovery
Snapshot test in `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/report.test.ts` covers all verdict types

narrative.Action:
Add `pan admin hygiene scan` as a new admin subgroup. Wire `src/cli/commands/admin/hygiene-handler.ts` and register it from `src/cli/commands/admin/index.ts`. The handler:

1. Refuses to run if `process.cwd()` matches `workspaces/feature-*` (force operator to invoke from main repo root — see hazard H10).
2. Builds `ClassifierContext` once (open PRs via `gh pr list --json`, worktrees via `listWorktrees`, tmux sessions via `listSessionNames`).
3. Iterates every `feature/*` branch (local + remote, deduped) and every `workspaces/feature-*` worktree; calls the classifiers.
4. Calls `generateCleanupReport` and prints `Wrote <path>` to stdout.
5. Exits 0 even when there's nothing to do (idempotent).

Flags: `--project <key>` (multi-project support; defaults to current repo), `--no-pr-check` (skip gh PR query for offline runs).
narrative.Tests: Integration test that runs the CLI against a tmp git repo with two branches and checks the report file appears.

subItems.title: `pan admin hygiene scan --help` lists `--project` and `--no-pr-check` flags
Running from a `workspaces/feature-*` directory exits non-zero with an error explaining to run from the main repo
`gh pr list` is called exactly once per scan (cached for the duration)
Report file path is printed to stdout on success

narrative.Action:
Add `pan admin hygiene apply [--category <name>] [--dry-run] [--max <N>] [--force]` as a sibling CLI to `scan`. Behaviour:

1. Same `process.cwd()` refusal as scan (must run from main repo root).
2. Loads today's report; aborts with an error if no scan ran today.
3. For each candidate matching `--category` (or all categories if omitted), RE-RUNS the classifier (does not trust the report — see hazard H3).
4. If verdict has changed since the report, skips and logs.
5. For branches still `safe-to-delete`:
   - Log resolved SHA via `emitActivityEntrySync({source: 'hygiene', level: 'info', message: 'Deleting feature/pan-XXX at <sha>; reflog has 90 days'})`.
   - `git branch -D <branch>` (local), then `git push origin --delete <branch>` (remote). Ignore 'not found' errors on either side.
6. For worktrees still `safe-to-remove`:
   - If `dirty`, create `salvageable:<issue>:<iso-timestamp>:auto-sweep` stash inside the worktree first.
   - `git worktree remove --force <path>`.
7. After all per-worktree removes complete, run `git worktree prune` once.
8. `--dry-run` runs steps 3-4 but logs intentions instead of executing 5-7.
9. `--max <N>` caps deletions per category (default unlimited for CLI; janitor uses this with its own default).
10. Without `--force`, requires `--category` to be specified (don't accidentally nuke everything).
11. Writes a summary line per cleaned item to a `docs/CLEANUP-<date>-applied.log` audit file.
narrative.Tests: Integration tests in `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/apply.test.ts` against a fixture repo: dry-run reports correct counts; live run deletes only safe-to-delete and skips a branch whose classification changed mid-run; salvageable stash created for dirty worktrees.

subItems.title: `--dry-run` performs classifier re-check but executes NO destructive ops (verified by git log + worktree list unchanged after run)
Classifier is re-run inside apply; a branch whose verdict has changed since the report is skipped with a log line
Dirty worktree → `salvageable:<issue>:<iso-ts>:auto-sweep` stash created BEFORE `git worktree remove`; stash ref logged
Every destructive op writes an `emitActivityEntrySync` entry with the resolved SHA (for branches) or workspace path (for worktrees)
Audit log at `docs/CLEANUP-<date>-applied.log` appended on every live run; idempotent across multiple invocations on the same day
Without `--category` AND without `--force`, exits with a usage error (don't accidentally nuke everything)
`git worktree prune` runs exactly once at the end of an apply run, after all per-worktree removes

items.title: Flip `close_out` config defaults: delete branch + workspace by default

narrative.Action: In `src/lib/cloister/config.ts`, change `DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.close_out` from `{ remove_workspace: false, delete_feature_branch: false }` to `{ remove_workspace: true, delete_feature_branch: true }`. Update `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/config.test.ts` snapshot. Add a brief note in `docs/PANOPTICON_DEV_SOP.md` (or `docs/HYGIENE.md` if that exists post-bead-hygiene-docs) explaining the change and how to opt out per-project via `projects.yaml`.

subItems.title: `DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.close_out` has `remove_workspace: true` and `delete_feature_branch: true`
Existing config tests updated; `npm test -- src/lib/cloister/__tests__/config.test.ts` passes
Migration note added to project docs explaining the change and the per-project opt-out

narrative.Action:
After `teardownWorkspace` returns in `src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts:closeOut`, add a post-condition step:

1. If `closeOutConfig?.delete_feature_branch !== false`: assert `git branch --list feature/<issue-lower>` is empty AND `git ls-remote --heads origin feature/<issue-lower>` returns nothing. If either still shows the branch, emit `close_out_invariant_violated` activity entry (level: warn, with the lingering ref).
2. If `closeOutConfig?.remove_workspace !== false`: assert no `workspaces/feature-<issue-lower>` directory exists AND `git worktree list` does not include it. Same loud-but-non-blocking activity entry on violation.
3. The post-condition is logged in the workflow result as a separate `StepResult` so the dashboard surfaces violations without aborting the workflow (issue close + label apply must still proceed — a leftover branch shouldn't block tracker closure).
narrative.Tests: Unit test against a mocked closeOut where teardown is stubbed to leave branches behind; assert the warn activity entry is emitted and the workflow still returns success: true.

subItems.title: Post-condition checks run AFTER teardownWorkspace and BEFORE closeIssue in workflows.ts:closeOut
Violations emit `close_out_invariant_violated` activity entry with the lingering ref's name and SHA
Violation does NOT abort the workflow; closeIssue still runs (loud-but-non-blocking)
Post-condition is skipped when `delete_feature_branch: false` or `remove_workspace: false` is set in config (don't false-alarm operators who opted out)

narrative.Action:
Add a low-frequency patrol hook in `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:runPatrol`:

1. Gated on `cleanup.janitor.enabled` (default `true`) in cloister config.
2. Runs only once per `cleanup.janitor.interval_minutes` (default 60) — track `state.lastJanitor` ISO timestamp in deacon state; skip if last run was less than the interval ago.
3. First-run guard: if `app_settings.janitor.armed !== true`, the run is forced into dry-run mode. Emits one `janitor_dry_run` activity entry listing first 20 candidates and pointing to the operator UI for arming.
4. When armed: invokes the apply CLI logic in-process (reuse `applyCleanup({ category: 'all', max: cleanup.janitor.max_per_cycle, dryRun: false })`) with a hard cap of 10 deletions per cycle (`cleanup.janitor.max_per_cycle` default 10).
5. Excess candidates logged via `janitor_queued` activity entry with their count.
6. Honors `isDeaconGloballyPaused()` (skips the whole run).
7. Each janitor decision is logged via the existing deacon `addLog` channel for retrievability through the dashboard.
narrative.Tests: Vitest with `vi.useFakeTimers()` to advance patrol cycles; mock the apply function; verify (a) first run is dry-run without armed flag, (b) subsequent runs respect interval, (c) max_per_cycle cap is honored.

subItems.title: Patrol respects `cleanup.janitor.interval_minutes` and does NOT run on every 60s tick
First run without `app_settings.janitor.armed === true` is forced into dry-run and emits `janitor_dry_run` activity entry
`cleanup.janitor.max_per_cycle` caps deletions per cycle (default 10); excess emits `janitor_queued`
Janitor reuses `applyCleanup` from the hygiene module — no duplicate destructive logic in deacon.ts
Skipped entirely when `isDeaconGloballyPaused()` returns true
Tests use `vi.useFakeTimers()` per CLAUDE.md (no real-time waits)

narrative.Action: Create `skills/pan-admin-hygiene/SKILL.md` per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md. Documents the `pan admin hygiene scan` and `pan admin hygiene apply` flags (including the safety filters, `--category`, `--dry-run`, `--force`, `--max`), the `cleanup:keep` git note escape hatch, and the operator workflow (scan → review report → apply per category). Must pass `scripts/lint-skills.sh` (cross-checked against actual CLI help output). This is required because the lint script enforces 'when CLI changes, wrapper skill changes in the same commit'.

subItems.title: Skill file at `skills/pan-admin-hygiene/SKILL.md` exists and matches the shape in docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md
Every flag the skill mentions appears in `pan admin hygiene scan --help` or `pan admin hygiene apply --help` output (verified by lint-skills.sh)
`npm run lint` passes (lint-skills.sh finds no skill ↔ CLI drift)

narrative.Action:
Create `docs/HYGIENE.md` covering:

1. Why this exists (the 2026-05-25 audit, 9 stale branches after close-out).
2. The lifecycle of a feature branch: created → merged → close-out (deletes by default) → janitor catches strays.
3. How to run a manual sweep: `pan admin hygiene scan` then `pan admin hygiene apply --category <safe-to-delete|safe-to-remove>`.
4. Safety filters reference table (every verdict, what triggers it).
5. Escape hatches: `cleanup:keep` git note (permanent pin), `close_out` per-project override in projects.yaml.
6. Recovery: reflog for branches (90 days), salvageable:* stash for worktrees.
7. Janitor configuration: `cleanup.janitor.*` keys, the `app_settings.janitor.armed` toggle, how to first-arm after deploy.

Add a one-line pointer in CLAUDE.md's 'Project Structure' section (next to Stash Hygiene).
narrative.Tests: Manual read-through; lint-skills.sh references this doc.

subItems.title: `docs/HYGIENE.md` exists and covers scan → report → apply → janitor flow end-to-end
Safety filters reference table lists every classifier verdict (both branch + worktree) with what triggers it
Recovery section documents reflog (branches) and salvageable stash (worktrees)
CLAUDE.md has a 1-line pointer to docs/HYGIENE.md

items.title: End-to-end test suite for hygiene module

narrative.Action:
Aggregate test coverage check across the hygiene module. Files expected (some may already exist from the per-bead unit tests above; this bead enforces the e2e harness and completeness):

1. `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/branch-classifier.test.ts` — already from `branch-classifier` bead, verify it exists.
2. `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/worktree-classifier.test.ts` — from `worktree-classifier` bead.
3. `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/report.test.ts` — from `hygiene-report` bead.
4. `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/apply.test.ts` — from `hygiene-cli-apply` bead.
5. `src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/e2e.test.ts` — NEW: scaffolds a tmp git repo with 5 branches (one in each verdict) and 3 worktrees, runs scan + apply --dry-run, asserts report matches and no destructive ops happen; then runs apply (live), asserts only safe-to-delete branches + safe-to-remove worktrees are gone.
6. `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-janitor.test.ts` — from `deacon-janitor` bead.
7. `src/lib/lifecycle/__tests__/closeout-postcondition.test.ts` — from `closeout-postcondition` bead.

All tests must use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for any retry/delay logic (per CLAUDE.md). Run `npm test` from workspace root after `bun install`; must pass.

subItems.title: All 7 test files listed above exist and pass
`src/lib/hygiene/__tests__/e2e.test.ts` exercises scan → dry-run → live-apply against a tmp fixture repo
`npm test` passes from workspace root after `bun install`
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

items.title: Run the one-shot sweep against the live repo

narrative.Action:
After all tooling beads land, execute the actual sweep. This is the operator-facing deliverable that closes the issue:

1. Pull latest main; run `pan admin hygiene scan` from the main repo root.
2. Inspect `docs/CLEANUP-<today>.md`. Commit the report.
3. Run `pan admin hygiene apply --category safe-to-delete --dry-run` and review output.
4. Run `pan admin hygiene apply --category safe-to-delete` (live).
5. Run `pan admin hygiene apply --category safe-to-remove --dry-run` (worktrees), review.
6. Run `pan admin hygiene apply --category safe-to-remove` (live).
7. Run `pan admin hygiene scan` again — confirm report now shows ~20 or fewer feature branches and only worktrees with active agents.
8. Commit the final report + audit log with `chore: hygiene sweep 2026-05-25 — N branches, M worktrees removed` (numbers from the audit log).

IMPORTANT: This bead is destructive. The work agent MUST NOT execute `apply` (live) without inspecting the dry-run output first. If any branch in the report looks wrong, halt and flag to the operator instead of forcing the delete.
narrative.Tests: Post-sweep validation: `git branch -l 'feature/*' | wc -l` < 30; `git worktree list | wc -l` ≤ 1 + (count of running work agents).

subItems.title: `docs/CLEANUP-<today>.md` committed with classification of all 194+ local feature branches and 28 worktrees
Dry-run output reviewed BEFORE live apply for each category
Post-sweep: `git branch -l 'feature/*' | wc -l` returns under 30
Post-sweep: every remaining worktree has either an open PR, a running agent, or recent commits (<7 days)
No `salvageable:*` stashes were dropped during the sweep (verify via `git stash list | grep salvageable:` count matches pre-sweep count + any new stashes the sweep created)
Audit log `docs/CLEANUP-<today>-applied.log` committed alongside the report
